verses.text: 'Praise the LORD, O my soul,' David vows to praise Him 'while I live' and sing 'while I have any being.' Praise is to fill the whole of life. As long as we have breath, we have reason and duty to bless God.

'Praise the LORD, O my soul,' David vows to praise Him 'while I live' and sing 'while I have any being.' Praise is to fill the whole of life. As long as we have breath, we have reason and duty to bless God.

'Put not your trust in princes, nor in the son of man, in whom there is no help,' for at death his thoughts perish. Human help is frail and fleeting. To lean on mortal man is to lean on what death soon removes.

'Put not your trust in princes, nor in the son of man, in whom there is no help,' for at death his thoughts perish. Human help is frail and fleeting. To lean on mortal man is to lean on what death soon removes.

Happy rather is he whose help and hope are in 'the LORD his God,' Maker of all, who keeps truth, judges the oppressed, feeds the hungry, and frees the prisoner. He is the sure helper. The God who made all things cares for all who are wronged.

Happy rather is he whose help and hope are in 'the LORD his God,' Maker of all, who keeps truth, judges the oppressed, feeds the hungry, and frees the prisoner. He is the sure helper. The God who made all things cares for all who are wronged.

Happy rather is he whose help and hope are in 'the LORD his God,' Maker of all, who keeps truth, judges the oppressed, feeds the hungry, and frees the prisoner. He is the sure helper. The God who made all things cares for all who are wronged.

The LORD opens blind eyes, raises the bowed, loves the righteous, and upholds the stranger and orphan, while He 'shall reign for ever.' His mercy and reign are endless. The eternal King is the friend of the weak and the lowly.
